name: Winter Melon Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 large dried mushrooms, soaked and cut into 1/4 inch squares
1/3 cup diced roasted pork or roasted duck meat
1 boned, skinless chicken breast, cut into 1/4 inch cubes
1/3 cup abalone, cut into 1/4 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the stem end of the melon and take out the seeds and soft pulp.
2. Place a piece of cheesecloth under the melon to make removal easier after cooking.
3. Put the melon in a pot large enough to hold it.
4. Add the soup ingredients into the cavity of the melon.
5. Steam the melon with its soup ingredients for 1 hour, ensuring there is enough water in the pot for steaming.
6. Add the diced roasted pork or duck meat and steam an additional 15 minutes.
7. Mix the cubed chicken with cornstarch and salt, then add it to the hot broth and stir so the pieces do not stick together. Steam an additional 15 minutes.
8. Add the shrimp mixture, straw mushrooms, abalone, and turn off the heat.
9. Grasp the corners of the cheesecloth and gently lift out the melon onto a large round serving platter.
10. Remove the cheesecloth and garnish with coriander.
11. Serve hot, carefully scooping out the melon pulp to avoid puncturing the shell and preventing soup from leaking out.
This dish requires careful preparation but is always appreciated by family and guests. The melon serves as a container for the soup and can be decorated with figures, Chinese characters, or geometric patterns to resemble a ceramic piece. Alternatively, a trivet or flat metal piece with strings attached can support the melon during cooking. Immersing the melon in water up to its top ensures better cooking control. The top of the melon cut off can be used in the soup or another dish.
